behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure is at a module boundary (manifest parser -> dependency graph resolver -> vulnerability check input builder), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess is required to catch the core failure; fixture manifests/lock data are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: System is rejected because behavior text only requires extraction/inclusion logic, not real HTTP/subprocess/filesystem-event behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner components together against fixture manifests with known direct+transitive trees and assert the vulnerability-query input contains the full expected dependency set (names + resolved versions).

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this behavior is about real API query semantics for version ranges; mocks can hide mismatches in server-side range handling. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed API could always return correct fixtures, missing real contract/encoding issues. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because behavior text explicitly targets query-result correctness against version ranges, which depends on real API behavior, not only local wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real vulnerability API endpoint using dependencies whose package name has vulnerabilities outside and inside the manifest version range; assert only in-range CVEs are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is deterministic ordering logic in one comparator/sort function. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because no module handoff is required by behavior text; it is pure ranking logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorting function with mixed-severity CVE fixtures and assert strict ordering by severity rank, then descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when parser/editor/serializer modules interact (targeted edit while preserving unrelated entries). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with local fixture files; no real network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System is rejected because behavior text does not require OS-specific file-event behavior or external services.
behaviors.test_description: Apply the update pipeline to a fixture manifest containing many dependencies and formatting variants; assert output changes exactly one dependency version and is byte-identical for all other entries/sections.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires creation on a real repository (real git host/API). MOCK-HIDE: mocked PR client can pass while auth, branch, diff, or API contract fails in reality. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by explicit text 'real repository'.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner end-to-end against a real test repository and verify a PR is actually opened with a diff containing only the intended dependency version bump.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is pure semver comparison logic, typically one function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because behavior text focuses on semver rule evaluation, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the semver classifier with cases like 1.x->2.x (breaking) and 1.2->1.3 (non-breaking), including pre-release/build metadata edge cases.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in orchestration/state handoff (semver decision -> PR pipeline suppression -> reporting output). REAL-INFRA: no real network is required to prove skip+flag behavior; an in-process PR client spy and reporter are enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System is rejected because behavior text does not require successful real PR API interaction, only that it is skipped and flagged.
behaviors.test_description: Wire semver checker, PR service interface, and report generator; trigger a breaking bump and assert PR creation is not invoked while scanner output contains the review-required flag.
